finding: Strict experiments require an immutable policy artifact instead of silent policy fallback.
evidence_or_action: Policy ID/path/SHA/schema binding is stored per experiment; changing the global active policy never mutates an existing experiment binding.

finding: Policy training and full CALO runtime are not one bit-identical transition implementation.
evidence_or_action: The native 32-feature training environment exposes v4/v5 cognition semantics, but the lightweight PPO rollout environment remains separate. Real-optimizer Policy Qualification is mandatory.

finding: The policy could not observe HPEM/readiness/precision/variable-group state.
evidence_or_action: The native 32-feature policy schema exposes eight bounded v4.1+ cognition features and is retained unchanged in v5 for checkpoint compatibility.

finding: No formal policy qualification gate existed.
evidence_or_action: Candidate vs reference vs No-AI paired qualification, qualification history, grades, and immutable SHA provenance are implemented.

finding: Long-running policy evolution needs latest-vs-best checkpoint management.
evidence_or_action: v5 policy lineages, cumulative epochs, immutable deployable checkpoints, latest/best-qualified roles, forks, exact resume, and continuation phases are implemented. Qualification scheduling is suggested but not automatically launched at every configured interval.

finding: The bundled legacy policy has no demonstrated native ORPD superiority.
evidence_or_action: Legacy/unqualified policies remain labeled honestly. No policy becomes scientifically preferred until recorded paired qualification demonstrates it.

finding: AI/rule/online-credit authority weights are not established as universally optimal.
evidence_or_action: Use paired IEEE 30/57 development ablations and freeze before IEEE 118/300 holdout evaluation.

finding: CALO does not yet use the same fully Torch-native optimizer-control path as Torch baseline optimizers.
evidence_or_action: The scientific evaluator is accelerator-native, but a complete CUDA/XPU CALO-control rewrite remains deferred until seeded scientific parity can be proven.

finding: CALO cognitive/memory control state remains primarily compact NumPy host state.
evidence_or_action: v5 does not falsely claim full device-resident CALO control; this remains a separate performance/scientific-parity project.

finding: Per-learner candidate decisions still include Python-level logic.
evidence_or_action: Bounded tensor-shaped memories and reused 3D scratch reduce overhead while preserving seeded behavior; grouped device kernels remain future work.

finding: CUDA policy inference still materializes small action arrays on the host for NumPy CALO control.
evidence_or_action: Elimination depends on the future device-resident CALO control path.

finding: Evaluator warm-up could be mistaken for end-to-end CALO throughput.
evidence_or_action: Evaluator calibration is labeled explicitly; CALO metadata records policy, control/candidate, evaluator, and learning-update timings separately.

finding: Persistent exact-evaluation caching could cost more than it saves at low hit rates.
evidence_or_action: Within-batch exact dedup remains; persistent cross-batch cache auto-disables after measured low reuse while FE accounting is unchanged.

finding: HPEM/context/success-memory operations still include small host-side loops.
evidence_or_action: Bounded memories keep cost controlled; further device kernels require parity-qualified implementation.

finding: Cross-run policy broker can add latency for an isolated single CALO run.
evidence_or_action: Retain it for multi-run batching; add a direct fast path only after end-to-end profiling shows a reproducible benefit.

finding: Workflow/setup state was not restored when reopening experiments.
evidence_or_action: ExperimentWorkspaceRestorer restores authoritative config, workflow access, policy binding, intelligence selections, historical plots, and lightweight view state.

finding: Old FE-horizon evidence could be overwritten or silently mixed after extensions.
evidence_or_action: Run-horizon snapshots, revision records, horizon-aware statistics/results/export, validation-at-horizon records, and revision-specific export folders preserve evidence boundaries.

finding: A crash can lose live telemetry after the last committed optimizer checkpoint.
evidence_or_action: CALO exact run checkpoints preserve optimization state for continuation, but every transient/downsampled GUI telemetry point between checkpoints is not guaranteed to survive a hard crash.

finding: Policy training could not continue safely across sessions or completed targets.
evidence_or_action: Exact resume retains model, optimizer, RNG, curriculum/history and blocks scientific hyperparameter drift; cumulative, additional, and indefinite modes are supported.

finding: More policy epochs could destructively replace a better earlier policy.
evidence_or_action: Immutable lineage checkpoints keep latest and best-qualified roles separate; experiments remain bound to their original checkpoint SHA.

finding: Exact same-trajectory FE-horizon continuation is not available for every optimizer.
evidence_or_action: CALO has full exact optimizer-state continuation. For multi-algorithm publication comparisons, v5 provides paired recompute-from-original-seed at the new horizon; unsupported baselines are never mislabeled as exact continuation.

finding: Post-hoc extension of only good-looking runs could bias primary statistics.
evidence_or_action: All-paired and predeclared deterministic-subset protocols are publication-eligible; manual/post-hoc selection is explicitly exploratory and excluded from unbiased primary claims.

finding: A later longer exploratory branch could redefine or destroy the primary comparison horizon.
evidence_or_action: Primary completed publication-eligible revisions are tracked independently of exploratory branches; later paired revisions can branch from the correct primary/source horizon.

finding: Exact continuation always resumed whichever branch happened to be current.
evidence_or_action: v5 records/selects an explicit source FE horizon and resolves the checkpoint belonging to that preserved run segment; revision-scoped output paths prevent checkpoint overwrite.

finding: A segmented 5k→10k continuation could be incorrectly presented as identical to a run planned for 10k from FE=0.
evidence_or_action: Trajectory semantics are recorded explicitly. Exact continuation is labeled segmented; recompute-from-seed is the publication-safe from-start higher-horizon trajectory for paired comparisons.

finding: Configured policy qualification intervals do not automatically execute expensive qualification campaigns during training.
evidence_or_action: The interval is currently advisory. Automatic asynchronous qualification/promotion requires a separately budgeted, non-training validation scheduler to avoid leakage and resource contention.
